referenceSource.title: Staging and Grading Periodontitis
referenceSource.workshop: 2017 World Workshop on the Classification of Periodontal and Peri-Implant Diseases and Conditions

diabetes.details: Type 2 Diabetes detected. Currently managed with Metformin (500 mg). Notes: Managed with diet and Metformin.
diabetes.gradingImpact: If HbA1c < 7.0%: no grade modification. If HbA1c >= 7.0%: shifts to Grade C (rapid progression risk).

smoking.source: No smoking indicators found in medical history
smoking.details: No smoking history detected. Non-smoker assumed based on available data.
smoking.gradingImpact: Non-smoker profile consistent with Grade A.

medicationFlags.name: Amlodipine
medicationFlags.relevance: Calcium channel blockers may cause gingival overgrowth

allergyFlags.name: Penicillin
allergyFlags.relevance: Common first-line antibiotic for periodontal infections — alternative needed

allergyFlags.name: Latex
allergyFlags.relevance: Latex-free gloves and equipment required for periodontal procedures

allergyFlags.name: Ibuprofen
allergyFlags.relevance: NSAIDs commonly used post-procedure — alternative analgesic needed

reasoning: Diabetes detected — grade depends on HbA1c level (< 7.0% = Grade B, >= 7.0% = Grade C).
Note: Definitive grading requires clinical probing data, radiographs, and longitudinal bone loss measurements.

stagingReference.description: Staging classifies severity and extent of disease based on tissue destruction and complexity of management.
criteria_priority: Radiographic Bone Loss (RBL) if CAL unavailable

radiographic_bone_loss.location: Extending to middle third of root and beyond
Extending to middle third of root and beyond

gradingReference.description: Grading estimates rate of progression, response to therapy, and systemic impact.

Grade A.case_phenotype: Heavy biofilm deposits with low levels of destruction

Grade C.case_phenotype: Destruction exceeds expectations given biofilm deposits; patterns of rapid progression or early onset

clinicalNotes: Diabetes risk factor: Type 2 Diabetes detected. Currently managed with Metformin (500 mg). Notes: Managed with diet and Metformin.
Medication alert — Amlodipine: Calcium channel blockers may cause gingival overgrowth
Allergy alert — Penicillin: Common first-line antibiotic for periodontal infections — alternative needed
Allergy alert — Latex: Latex-free gloves and equipment required for periodontal procedures
Allergy alert — Ibuprofen: NSAIDs commonly used post-procedure — alternative analgesic needed
